Recent papers

Recent papers on the Riemann hypothesis, surfaced by the prominence of their authors in this directory.

Showing 15 papers, ranked by the Top 100 standing of their authors then by recency. Authors who appear in the Top 100 are shown in bold.

#TitleAuthorsPublishedBest rank
1Short mollifiers of the Riemann zeta-functionJ. Brian Conrey, David W. Farmer, Chung-Hang Kwan, Yongxiao Lin, Caroline L. Turnage-Butterbaugh2025-08#2
2An update on the Linnik--Goldbach and Romanov problemsDaniel R. Johnston, Tim Trudgian2026-05#3
3Zero-free regions inspired by work of Heath-BrownChiara Bellotti, Tim Trudgian, Andrew Yang2026-03#3
4A round of Pintz to celebrate oscillations in sumsDaniel R. Johnston, Tim Trudgian2025-11#3
5The determination of norm-Euclidean cyclic cubic fieldsGustav Kjærbye Bagger, Andrew R. Booker, Bryce Kerr, Kevin McGown, Valeriia Starichkova, Tim Trudgian2025-07#3
6On the error term of the fourth moment of the Riemann zeta-functionNeea Palojärvi, Tim Trudgian2025-06#3
7Centered Moments of Weighted One-Level Densities of $GL(2)$ $L$-FunctionsLawrence Dillon, Xiaoyao Huang, Say-Yeon Kwon, Meiling Laurence, Steven J. Miller, Vishal Muthuvel, Luke Rowen, Pramana Saldin, Steven Zanetti2025-09#4
8The $θ= \infty$ Conjecture and the Riemann Hypothesis for Automorphic $L$-functionsAnji Dong, Nawapan Wattanawanichkul, Alexandru Zaharescu2026-05#5
9Binomial coefficients with divisors avoiding an intervalHung M. Bui, Kyle Pratt, Alexandru Zaharescu2026-05#5
10Zeros of Polynomials in Derivatives of Automorphic $L$-functionsAnji Dong, Nawapan Wattanawanichkul, Alexandru Zaharescu2025-12#5
11Simultaneous non-vanishing of Dirichlet L-functionsHung M. Bui, Alexandra Florea, Micah B. Milinovich2026-04#6
12Amplified Fourth Moment of the Riemann Zeta-Function and ApplicationsHung M. Bui, Richard R. Hall, Martin Subira Jorge2025-11#6
13Simultaneous nonvanishing of Dirichlet $L$-functions in Galois orbitsHung M. Bui, Alexandra Florea, Hieu T. Ngo2025-07#6
14Zeta Zeros in a Narrow Vertical BoxDaniel A. Goldston, Ade Irma Suriajaya2026-03#11
15Zeta Zeros on the Critical LineDaniel A. Goldston, Ade Irma Suriajaya2025-11#11

Most cited (all time)

The ten most-cited Mathematics works on the Riemann hypothesis, by citation count in OpenAlex. Results are filtered to the Mathematics field (OpenAlex field 26) and ranked by total citations.

The Top 100 ranking is designed to capture prominent, currently active researchers. If an author in this table is not linked to a profile, it is usually because they are deceased or less active than the researchers in the ranking, not because they are less significant to the field.

#TitleAuthorsYearCitations
1The Theory of the Riemann Zeta-FunctionE. C. Titchmarsh; D. R. Heath‐Brown19873,273
2The Riemann Zeta FunctionAnatolij A. Karatsuba; Melvyn B. Nathanson1992904
3Contributions to the theory of the riemann zeta-function and the theory of the distribution of primesG. H. Hardy; J. E. Littlewood1916454
4Limit Theorems for the Riemann Zeta-FunctionAntanas Laurinčikas1996376
5THEOREM ON THE “UNIVERSALITY” OF THE RIEMANN ZETA-FUNCTIONСергей Воронин1975344
6Trace formula in noncommutative geometry and the zeros of the Riemann zeta functionAlain Connes1999342
7Distribution functions and the Riemann zeta functionBørge Jessen; Aurel Wintner1935331
8Mean-Value Theorems in the Theory of the Riemann Zeta-FunctionA. E. Ingham1928277
9Decoupling, exponential sums and the Riemann zeta functionJean Bourgain2016264
10Spectral Theory of the Riemann Zeta-FunctionYoichi Motohashi1997262
